task_completion.description: Navigate to the Ngorongoro Crater location on the map
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map viewport shows the distinctive circular Ngorongoro Crater in Tanzania (approx -3.17, 35.59).

task_completion.description: Draw a polygon tracing the crater floor
task_completion.visual_evidence: A polygon shape is visible on the map, roughly following the inner rim/boundary of the crater floor.

task_completion.description: Ensure the polygon has sufficient detail (at least 15 vertices)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The polygon boundary shows many control points (dots) or follows the curvature closely, indicating >15 vertices rather than a simple square or triangle.
The 'Name' field in the polygon properties dialog reads 'Ngorongoro Crater Floor'.

task_completion.description: Set fill color to green and outline color to red
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Style/Color' settings show Green for Area/Fill and Red for Lines/Outline, or the polygon on the map appears green with a red border.

task_completion.description: Set line width to 2.0 or greater
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Width' field in the Line/Style settings is set to 2.0 or a higher number.

task_completion.description: Save the file as KML to the correct path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or file dialog shows the filename 'ngorongoro_habitat.kml' and the folder path includes 'Documents'.

integrity.description: Agent used a mapping application GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a mapping application like Google Earth Pro or Marble, not a text editor or terminal for the primary task.
Mouse cursor interacting with map tools, placing points, or adjusting settings in dialog boxes.

integrity.description: Agent did not generate the KML via script
integrity.visual_evidence: No evidence of writing Python/Bash scripts to generate the KML file directly without visual confirmation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Ngorongoro Crater is a large, circular volcanic caldera located at approximately -3.1616 latitude and 35.5878 longitude. The crater floor covers an area of roughly 260 square kilometers. The polygon should trace the flat inner floor of this circular feature.
